div.h1: Iteration Memory Access: Sparse Set vs Archetype
div.h3: Cache Locality Comparison for 100K Entity Query

div.p: Legend: 🟢 Green = Cache-friendly (sequential) 🔴 Red = Cache-hostile (random/indirection) 🟡 Yellow = Control flow / Mixed

ul.li: Pre-loads cache lines ahead of access
Processes 8 entities per cache line load
2 dense accesses: 8-20 cycles 100K entities: ~8.8-22M cycles (~3-7ms at 3GHz)
Zero indirection overhead 100K entities: ~0.8M cycles (~0.27ms at 3GHz)

div.p: 10-27x faster for iteration (Sparse set wins on add/remove)
Array Access (random) Position data at offset
Array Access (random) Velocity data at offset
Sequential Array Access positions + row × sizeof(Position)
Sequential Array Access velocities + row × sizeof(Velocity)
sparse[0..N] (8MB for 1M entities) [EMPTY][0][EMPTY][1][EMPTY][2]... Random access pattern ❌
positions[0..N] (contiguous) [P₀][P₁][P₂][P₃][P₄][P₅][P₆][P₇]... Sequential access ✓ (8 per cache line)
velocities[0..N] (contiguous) [V₀][V₁][V₂][V₃][V₄][V₅][V₆][V₇]... Sequential access ✓ (8 per cache line)
